title: Chicago Loop & Surrounds · 5 Days
subtitle: Chicago → Indiana Dunes → Starved Rock → Oak Park → Chicago

days.title: Arrive Chicago — Riverwalk & Millennium Park

stops.name: Millennium Park / Cloud Gate ('The Bean')
stops.note: Free to enter; The Bean is the iconic photo stop — go early before crowds.

stops.name: Chicago Riverwalk
stops.note: Walk east from Lake St bridge to Lake Shore Dr — best views of the skyline from the water level.

stops.name: Architecture Boat Tour (Chicago River)
stops.note: Book a 90-min Chicago Architecture Foundation cruise ahead — the best way to see 50+ landmark buildings from the water.

fuelCharging.name: Fuel @ O'Hare / Irving Park area
fuelCharging.note: Top off before heading into downtown where gas is pricier.

risks: Downtown Chicago parking is expensive ($30–50/day) — choose a hotel with parking or use a Lower Wacker garage to save.

days.title: Chicago Neighborhoods — Lincoln Park, Wicker Park, Pilsen

stops.name: Lincoln Park Zoo
stops.note: Free admission year-round. The lakefront path alongside is great for a morning walk or bike ride.

stops.name: Navy Pier
stops.note: Centennial Wheel ($18), Chicago Shakespeare Theater, IMAX — great lakefront stroll and views.

stops.name: Wicker Park / Bucktown
stops.note: Best indie shops, street art, and cocktail bars in the city. Walk Milwaukee Ave and Damen Ave.

stops.name: Pilsen Murals & National Museum of Mexican Art
stops.note: Free museum; the outdoor murals along 16th St and Blue Island Ave are world-class.

meal.name: Xoco by Rick Bayless, River North

risks: Use the CTA (L train/bus) or rideshare for neighborhood hopping today — parking in Wicker Park and Pilsen is extremely limited on weekends.

days.title: Day trip — Indiana Dunes National Park
days.to: Indiana Dunes, IN (return to Chicago overnight)

stops.name: Indiana Dunes National Park Visitor Center
stops.note: Pick up a trail map here. The dunes are taller than you'd expect — wear sunscreen and bring water.

stops.name: Mount Baldy Dune
stops.note: Tallest dune at 126 ft; ranger-guided 1.5-mi hike is required for the summit. Check nps.gov for tour times.

stops.name: West Beach
stops.note: Best swimming beach in the park. On clear days you can see the Chicago skyline across Lake Michigan — stunning sunset view.

fuelCharging.name: Fuel @ Portage / Merrillville, IN (I-94)
fuelCharging.note: Gas is noticeably cheaper in Indiana than downtown Chicago — fill up here on the way out or back.

risks: West Beach and Mount Baldy parking lots fill by 10 AM on summer weekends — arrive before 9 AM or take the South Shore rail line from Millennium Station.
Lake Michigan has strong rip currents and no lifeguards at several beaches — check NPS swim conditions before entering the water.

days.title: Day trip — Starved Rock State Park & Matthiessen

stops.name: Starved Rock State Park Visitor Center
stops.note: Pick up a canyon map. 18 sandstone canyons and Illinois River bluffs make this one of the best hiking spots in the Midwest.

stops.name: St. Louis Canyon
stops.note: Most spectacular canyon in the park — towering sandstone walls and a seasonal waterfall at the back.

stops.name: Starved Rock Bluff
stops.note: Short 0.25-mi trail to the historic 125-ft bluff top with sweeping Illinois River views. Named from a 1769 Illiniwek siege.

stops.name: Matthiessen State Park (adjacent)
stops.note: Less crowded than Starved Rock with equally beautiful canyon scenery — highly recommended add-on; free admission.

fuelCharging.name: Fuel @ Ottawa, IL (US-6 / I-80)
fuelCharging.note: Good fuel and food stop a few miles east of the parks.

risks: Canyon trails are rocky and can be slippery after rain — wear closed-toe shoes with good grip.
If you ever plan to stay overnight, Starved Rock Lodge fills months ahead on summer weekends — day-trip from Chicago is the easier option.

days.title: Oak Park (Frank Lloyd Wright) → Depart

stops.name: Frank Lloyd Wright Home & Studio, Oak Park
stops.note: America's densest collection of Wright architecture. Book a guided tour online at gowright.org — self-guided audio tours also available.

stops.name: Oak Park Historic District (walking tour)
stops.note: Dozens of Prairie-style homes visible from the sidewalk; the free OPRF audio app guides you past 20+ sites in under an hour.

stops.name: Ernest Hemingway Birthplace Museum, Oak Park
stops.note: Hemingway was born here in 1899. Pairs perfectly with the FLW tour for a full Oak Park cultural morning.

fuelCharging.name: Fuel @ Oak Park / I-290
fuelCharging.note: Fill up before heading to O'Hare to avoid rental-return fuel surcharges.

risks: I-290 and I-90/94 can back up badly during morning rush — leave Oak Park by 11 AM to reach O'Hare comfortably for a midday flight.

bookingCountdown.note: Summer slots sell out weeks ahead; early-morning or evening tours are cooler and less crowded.
August is peak summer season — rates and availability tighten fast.

bookingCountdown.item: Frank Lloyd Wright Home & Studio tour
bookingCountdown.note: Timed-entry tours; weekend slots book out fast in summer.

bookingCountdown.item: Shedd Aquarium / Museum of Science & Industry (optional rainy-day)
bookingCountdown.note: Timed tickets save long queues; worth adding if afternoon storms hit.

tips: Get a CTA Ventra card — the L train and buses link the Loop to every neighborhood and beat driving + parking every time.
The Chicago Architecture Center on Wacker Dr has a 1:400 scale skyline model and is worth $15 admission even before the boat tour.
Indiana Dunes and Starved Rock are free to enter — no annual pass needed unlike national parks.
August is prime thunderstorm season on the Great Lakes; build flexibility in the afternoon schedule, especially for the open-air parks.
Deep dish pizza takes 40 minutes to bake — call ahead or order online so it's ready when you arrive; Lou Malnati's, Giordano's, and Pequod's are the local favorites.
disclaimer: This itinerary was assembled by an AI from general knowledge and may be out of date. Hours, prices, park alerts, timed-entry rules, and road conditions change frequently — always confirm with official sources (nps.gov, dnr.illinois.gov, choosechicago.com) before and during your trip.
